stance: Defensive-neutral balanced advantage; value-plus-revision tilt to cheap energy/materials/PSU and selective quality, trimming the low-conviction tail and falling-revision value traps.
experience_note: This is an early round with no graded track record yet, so I am pre-registering what would change my mind. I am watching whether the value-plus-rising-revision cohort (cheap energy/materials/PSU + LIC/Muthoot) actually outperforms the falling-revision value traps I avoided — if PNB/HPCL/IOC/Tata Motors PV beat my energy/materials overweight over the next 1-3 months, my "avoid falling revisions" rule was wrong here. I am also watching the momentum blow-offs I skipped (Sterlite Tech, MTAR): if they keep compounding, my caution cost me. Conviction-IC is the honest test: did my biggest weights (ONGC, Chennai Petro, Grasim, LIC) actually become my best calls, or did I size noise? If the energy/materials value tilt fails as a bloc, that is a regime read error, not stock-picking, and I should cut the whole theme rather than individual names.
